title: Teaching and Professional Experience
text:
Teaching Experience




  
    Aug 2025 – Dec 2026 — Co-Instructor, University of Nebraska–Lincoln
    
      Co-instructor for ENSC 230 – Energy and the Environment: Economics and Policy.
      Delivered lectures on selected topics and provided academic support to students.
      Designed problem sets and quizzes and held regular office hours.
    
  

  
    Mar 2018 – Aug 2023 — Advisor, Pecege Institute (University of São Paulo)
    
      Advised MBA students in Project Management on thesis development.
      Guided projects on cost analysis, risk management, and economic feasibility.
      Supervised over 200 students.
    
  

  
    Mar 2019 – Dec 2019 — Teaching Assistant, University of São Paulo
    
      Supported undergraduate instruction in Introduction to Economics (B.Sc. in Agronomy).
    
  

  
    Mar 2013 – Jul 2017 — Teaching Assistant, Federal University of Viçosa
    
      Assisted in Microeconomic Theory (MSc), Mathematical Economics, and Econometrics.
      Supported both undergraduate and graduate instruction.
    
  






Professional Experience




  
    Sep 2019 – Aug 2023 — Researcher, CEPEA — University of São Paulo
    
      Led GDP and labor market analysis for Brazilian agribusiness.
      Coordinated data collection and produced quarterly analytical reports.
      Contributed to Producer Price Index estimation and sectoral price analysis.
    
  

  
    Mar 2018 – Aug 2023 — Associate Professional, Pecege Institute — University of São Paulo
    
      Provided applied economic and financial consulting for MBA projects.
      Produced technical and feasibility reports.
    
  






Technical Skills



Programming: R, Python, Stata

title: Felipe Almeida
text:
PhD Candidate, Agricultural Economics
    University of Nebraska–Lincoln

    
I am interested in research in agribusiness, examining both broader topics—such as labor market dynamics and GDP performance—and more specific issues related to crop and livestock production, with a focus on their economic and environmental dimensions. More recently, I have also focused on biofuels, analyzing their economic and environmental implications. In addition, I am currently working on a project on beef sustainability in Nebraska, with an emphasis on carbon intensity and other environmental footprints associated with feed inputs.




  
    Research Interests
  

  

    
      Agricultural, Livestock, and Environmental Economics
    

    
      Production Economics
    

    
      Agribusiness
    

  



    
    
      Download CV
